question.text: Rental income received by a REIT from its properties is treated how for taxation purposes?

answer.text: Taxable at the REIT level at 30%, exempt for unit-holders
Taxable at both REIT level and unit-holder level
Exempt at the REIT level under Section 10(23FCA), taxable in the hands of unit-holders
Exempt at both REIT level and unit-holder level

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Rental income enjoys pass-through status in REITs. It is exempt at the REIT level under Section 10(23FCA) of the Income Tax Act. The rental income is then taxable in the hands of the unit-holders as per their applicable tax slab. **The Trap:** "Taxable at REIT level at 30%" is tempting because rental income from property is typically taxable at the entity level for companies. The pass-through mechanism is what makes REITs unique -- income passes through to unit-holders without entity-level taxation. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series X-A covers basic taxation; Series X-B requires knowing the specific sections (10(23FCA)) and the pass-through mechanism for different income types. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The pass-through for rental income is a key selling point of REITs over direct real estate company shares -- no double taxation. But remind clients that this rental income IS taxable in their hands at their slab rate.
